messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Error in trio package due to removed package
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Error in trio package due to removed package
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor team,

I've just got a notification that checking/building my BioC package trio fails. The reason for the error is that a function in trio depends on a function from the package haplo.stats that was very recently removed from CRAN

https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/haplo.stats/index.html

Rewriting the function in trio so that it would not depend on the haplo.stats function would be hardly possible, in particular considering the BioConductor release schedule. Even though this function is not one of the main features of the trio package, it is part of a workflow. So simply removing (temporarily) this function would also be not that simple (and not a really good idea).

The haplo.stats package, however, is still available at github (and the function used from this package does not seem to cause the problem because of which haplo.stats was removed from CRAN).

I would therefore be very grateful if you would let me know if it would be possible to also use a package that is only available at github as Suggest or Import in a BioConductor package, and if yes, if it would be okay to do so for the next release (and to remove this dependency until the release in October/November).

Thanks a lot,
Holger

messages.body_plain:
We will not allow github only packages.  This is strictly against Bioconductor policy. All dependencies must be actively maintained on CRAN or Bioconductor.

Your package will not be deprecated given the short time frame and you will be able to consider options and fix the package over the next release time period as it will still show (albeit ERRORing/Broken) in release and devel. Just ensure any fix then is applied to both the release branch and devel branch of Bioconductor

Cheers,


Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

messages.body_plain:
? Wed, 1 Apr 2026 12:54:58 +0000
Holger Schwender via Bioc-devel <bioc-devel at r-project.org> ?????:

> Rewriting the function in trio so that it would not depend on the
> haplo.stats function would be hardly possible, in particular
> considering the BioConductor release schedule. Even though this
> function is not one of the main features of the trio package, it is
> part of a workflow. So simply removing (temporarily) this function
> would also be not that simple (and not a really good idea).

Meanwhile, you can also try to contact the haplo.stats maintainer and
tell them that

 * the failure is reproducible with a build of R configured with
   --disable-long-double, Apple hardware is not required
 * the comparison in haplo.stats:::anova.haplo.glmlist fails because
   sum(fit.gaus$haplo.post.info$post) is 220 + 3.410605e-13 instead of
   exactly 220
 * the check in the anova.haplo.glmlist method should include enough of
   a tolerance to accommodate rounding errors, or maybe just call
   round() if the numbers being compared should always be whole

Except for this one problem, the package is in a good shape and is
worth trying a CRAN resubmission.

-- 
Best regards,
Ivan

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] SeqArray build failing due to missing VariantAnnotation
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] SeqArray build failing due to missing VariantAnnotation
messages.body_plain:
It looks like the ERROR is actually because of your call to requireNamespace where it actually does not have a verbose argument and causes unexpected failures because of this.  I think if you remove the verbose argument it should work as expected. For consistency I would also recommend keeping the open bracket on the if ...

> if (!requireNamespace("VariantAnnotation", quietly = TRUE, verbose=FALSE)) {
    stop("Please install VariantAnnotation to use this function")
}
Error: Please install VariantAnnotation to use this function

> if (!requireNamespace("VariantAnnotation", quietly = TRUE)) {
    stop("Please install VariantAnnotation to use this function")
}
>




Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

SeqArray is failing to build on nebbiolo1, with the error

Quitting from SeqArray.Rmd:293-299 [unnamed-chunk-11]
Error in `seqAsVCF()`:
! Please install VariantAnnotation to use this function

It looks like VariantAnnotation is passing its own builds, so I don't
understand why this error is happening. Is it related to the ordering of

be installed prior to building the vignette, right?

thanks,
Stephanie Gogarten

We are moving forward with the 3.23 release schedule.  In roughly 3 hours the RELEASE_3_22 will be frozen and no longer accept commits.  Any changes or bug corrections that are not pushed up by this time will not be in the Bioconductor 3.22 version of the package as we do not allow updates to past/frozen releases.

https://bioconductor.org/developers/release-schedule/

You still may push to the Bioconductor devel branch at any time. The devel branch is considered a release candidate at this time;  Please do not push up major API changes and limit to debugging for clean build/check reports.

Cheers

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Error when relying on biomaRt function due to Ensembl server being down
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Error when relying on biomaRt function due to Ensembl server being down
messages.body_plain:
Does anyone knows a good way to deal with the error when using the biomaRt function (such as useMart see below) that relies on the Ensembl server working? Because it then throws an error that makes the whole package fail.
'useMart()' failed: error: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.

Error:https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/3.23/bioc-LATEST/DominoEffect/nebbiolo1-buildsrc.html
As it worked on kjohnson3 and this problem persisted since years, I assume the server was just temporarily down while buiding the package on linux and that this is not related to the OS.

Thanks a lot. Peter

	[[alternative HTML version deleted]]

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel]  Error when relying on biomaRt function due to Ensembl server being down
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel]  Error when relying on biomaRt function due to Ensembl server being down
messages.body_plain:
Hi Peter,

I don't know of a great solution right now. We're dependent on the 
upstream service availability and stability.

We're aware of the issue though and currently working on a solution for 
the next Bioconductor release (3.24 in ~6 months). I added minor changes 
in the very experimental remart package 
(https://github.com/Huber-group-EMBL/remart) which should address your 
use case as far as I can tell.

It still needs to be improved, better tested, go through review, etc. 
but if all goes according to plan, it should provide a much more stable 
and faster drop-in alternative to biomaRt.

Best wishes,

Hugo

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Bioconductor Developer Seminar – April 27: Development Plans for CVXR
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor developers,
Our second Bioconductor Developer Seminar Series session is coming up. This series aims to connect cutting-edge research with Bioconductor development.
Topic: Development Plans for CVXR
Date & Time:
April 27, 2026
9 PM CET / 3 PM EST / 12 PM PST
? Register for the Zoom link:
https://dfci.zoom.us/webinar/register/WN_Z0aFKypFT-e5DbzFlrqtBg#/registration

Abstract for the talk: CVXR is the R port of CVXPY and has a developer community of two. As a result, it has fallen behind CVXPY in features, but new AI development tools plus facilities in R offer hope of catching up. In this talk, we describe our plans and present data from some early experiments that sound promising. The talk will be quite interactive, and we welcome feedback from the seasoned CVXPY community.

*We?d also appreciate your feedback as we plan upcoming seminars and workshops. This is a chance to help shape sessions that are genuinely useful. Let us know what tools you?re using, what?s working, and what topics you?d like to see covered. Share here: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSf11TDEFFOS05ff0rms5PgDPhgpwohKh_wbdEvKNGinpgSWsQ/viewform

The Bioconductor Team is continuing to identify packages that will be deprecated in the next release to allow for the Bioconductor community to respond accordingly. This is the current list of deprecated packages for Bioc 3.23.
It should be noted, we did try to reach out to these package maintainers multiple times and they were either unresponsive or had emails bounce. We encourage anyone that is familiar with a package maintainer on this list to reach out to them and notify them directly. Packages can be un-deprecated if a maintainer fixes the package to build/check cleanly before the next release and requests un-deprecation on the bioc-devel at r-project.org mailing list

This two-day workshop provides hands-on training in Microbiome data analysis using Bioconductor packages. This workshop charges a ?100 registration fee and includes lunch and coffee breaks.

  *   Hackathon

This will be a Bioconductor-centric hackathon. The hackathon is free to attend.

Register for the workshop here: https://www.lyyti.fi/reg/EuroBioC_workshop

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Important Bioconductor Release 3.23
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Important Bioconductor Release 3.23
messages.body_plain:
The deadline for fixing packages in preparation for the release is next Friday April 24th. All packages should be installing, building, and checking without ERROR in the Bioconductor devel build report.  While you can continue to push changes past this date, this ensures all changes are reflected in at least one build report before the code freeze for release.  Packages that continue to show ERRORs in the build report after the release will be consideration for deprecation. We are on schedule to release Wednesday April 29th.

Please also remember to update your NEWS files for any information about changes to your packages during this last development cycle.  Bioconductor collates all package news files as part of the release announcement. If your NEWS file is not formatted correctly and not updated by this weekend, it will not be included in the release announcement.

For clarification:
The release 3.23 branch has not yet been created.  Please continue to update the devel branch.
Next week when the release occurs the core team will automatically create the RELEASE_3_23 branch on git.bioconductor.org but until that time the only branch accessible is devel.  Do NOT try to create a RELEASE_3_23 branch on your own; the core team will do this for you.  Bioconductor will also handle bumping the y of a package version x.y.z  on release day.

Cheers,



Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

? Fred Hutchinson Cancer Center
? $50 USD (registration confirmed upon payment)

2. Bioconductor Hackathon
? Seattle Children?s Research Institute
? Free
This collaborative hackathon will focus on developing or extending Bioconductor packages, improving documentation and workflows, and contributing to community-driven project ideas. Participation is open to all experience levels.
? Sign up via the hackathon interest form: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSeCs82nvIQfSHzeOhc9fzvMPg0-VTOT8aoGyFAW0T2glod_oQ/viewform

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel]  [EXT] Re: Installation Error on nebbiolo2 Due to CVXR Dependency
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel]  [EXT] Re: Installation Error on nebbiolo2 Due to CVXR Dependency
messages.body_plain:
Dear Huang,

I maintain a package that depends on ANCOMBC and saw the recent Bioc-devel thread regarding the CVXR build failure on nebbiolo1.

To help resolve this quickly, I have just submitted a Pull Request to your GitHub repository. The PR completely removes the CVXR dependency by replacing its single use-case in the trend test with the quadprog package.

quadprog solves the exact same constrained optimization problem but is a lightweight CRAN standard that does not require Rust, meaning it will compile flawlessly on all Bioconductor servers. I have tested it locally to ensure the results perfectly match your original implementation.

I hope you find this PR helpful to get ANCOMBC building smoothly again!

Best regards,

Francesc


De: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> en nombre de Huang Lin (Frederick) <huanglinfrederick at gmail.com>

I?m writing to follow up on my previous inquiry, as I am still receiving
the error on nebbiolo1.

Do you anticipate adding rustc support on nebbiolo1 in the future? Also, is
there anything I can do on my end to help resolve this issue?

Thank you so much for your time and assistance,

huanglinfrederick at gmail.com> wrote:

* installing the package to build vignettes

I've noticed there are at least some other packages with a similar pattern: they seem to build and check OK on all platforms, but timeout on vignette build on kunpeng2. Is there anything I should do?

Hi everyone,

Sorry for the delayed response. The release is always pretty hectic.

Since we're moving toward an R Universe build where the rust will be available in the container, I updated to a newer rust on nebbiolo1 yesterday, so ANCOMBC is now passing.

Andres Wokaty (they/them)

Waldron Lab at CUNY SPH
Bioconductor Core Team
________________________________
From: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> on behalf of Francesc Catala Moll via Bioc-devel <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Sent: Tuesday, April 21, 2026 4:03 AM
To: Huang Lin (Frederick) <huanglinfrederick at gmail.com>; Vincent Carey <stvjc at channing.harvard.edu>
Cc: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Subject: Re: [Bioc-devel] [EXT] Re: Installation Error on nebbiolo2 Due to CVXR Dependency

* External Sender - Proceed Cautiously with Links and Attachments. *

Dear Huang,

I maintain a package that depends on ANCOMBC and saw the recent Bioc-devel thread regarding the CVXR build failure on nebbiolo1.

To help resolve this quickly, I have just submitted a Pull Request to your GitHub repository. The PR completely removes the CVXR dependency by replacing its single use-case in the trend test with the quadprog package.

quadprog solves the exact same constrained optimization problem but is a lightweight CRAN standard that does not require Rust, meaning it will compile flawlessly on all Bioconductor servers. I have tested it locally to ensure the results perfectly match your original implementation.

I hope you find this PR helpful to get ANCOMBC building smoothly again!

Best regards,

Francesc


De: Bioc-devel <bioc-devel-bounces at r-project.org> en nombre de Huang Lin (Frederick) <huanglinfrederick at gmail.com>
Fecha: lunes, 13 de abril de 2026 a las 18:00
Para: Vincent Carey <stvjc at channing.harvard.edu>
CC: bioc-devel at r-project.org <bioc-devel at r-project.org>
Asunto: [EXT] Re: [Bioc-devel] Installation Error on nebbiolo2 Due to CVXR Dependency

Dear Vincent and BioC Team,

the error on nebbiolo1.

Do you anticipate adding rustc support on nebbiolo1 in the future? Also, is
there anything I can do on my end to help resolve this issue?

Thank you so much for your time and assistance,

yesterday.

snapshot is taken weekly for the workflows?

Jim

messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Orthogene devel check error impacting downstream packages
messages.body_plain:
Dear Bioconductor developers,

We would like to report that the current devel version of orthogene appears
to have an issue that is affecting downstream packages.

The package currently shows an error in the Bioconductor devel build
reports:
https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/orthogene/

This problem also propagates to our package BulkSignalR, which depends on
orthogene, and has resulted in build errors as well.

https://bioconductor.org/checkResults/devel/bioc-LATEST/BulkSignalR/

We wanted to flag this here in case it has a broader impact on dependent
packages before the upcoming devel freeze.

We have also contacted the orthogene maintainers directly.

Thank you for your attention.

Best regards,


--

messages.subject: [Bioc-devel] Last day to make commits before Bioconductor RELEASE 3.23
messages.subject_clean: [Bioc-devel] Last day to make commits before Bioconductor RELEASE 3.23
messages.body_plain:
Hello Bioconductor developers,

We are still on track for Bioconductor Release 3.23 for this Wednesday  April 29th.  This means that tonight is the last day to commit changes to the devel branch before creation of the RELEASE_3_23 versions of your packages.  And any changes you make to your packages at this point will not be reflected in a build report before we branch.

There will be a temporary code freeze tomorrow morning around 7 am (EST)  while the branching for RELEASE_3_23 occurs automatically by the core team.  You will receive emails when the freeze occurs and when commits to the branches can resume.

As clarification,  you will still be able to commit to the release 3.23 branch and the devel branch throughout the next release cycle. But any packages that have not been fixed for failures or bugs in devel would then have to be fixed/uploaded to both the RELEASE_3_23 branch and the devel branch after tomorrow morning. Any package that remains broken in release would also be candidates for deprecation in 3.24

Cheers,



Lori Kern

Bioconductor Core Team

Roswell Park Comprehensive Cancer Center

Department of Biostatistics & Bioinformatics

Elm & Carlton Streets

Buffalo, New York 14263

Hi,

The build error described below persists on kjohnson3. I can't reproduce it on my local Linux machine, and the package also installs without error on nebbiolo1. It looks like it may be a conflict between the LaTeX file emitted by RMarkdown and the LaTeX programs installed on kjohnson3. I don't know if there's anything I can do at my end to resolve this.

- tyler
